source_priority: temp workbook as derived case ledger
workbook_rules: do not treat the workbook as the product source of truth
derive expected behavior from the WRCF PDFs first
use the workbook to map PDF-defined behavior into stable test IDs and coverage
if an Excel/workbook step is clear, follow it directly during test execution design
if spec behavior drifts from a clear workbook step, fix the spec instead of reinterpreting the step
ask for human intervention only when the workbook step itself is ambiguous, wrong, or conflicts with product intent
when a user request has ambiguous scope, restate the intended action and ask for confirmation before executing
when a user request or source artifact implies a specific environment, restate the environment you plan to use and ask for confirmation before validating or rerunning if there is any ambiguity
do not silently narrow or expand the requested scope based on recent context
if Excel libraries are unavailable, read the xlsx zip/xml payload directly
for temp/WRCF End-to-End Test Cases_reverified-v2.xlsx, do not add a new Test run column unless the user explicitly asks for it
if the user does not explicitly ask for a new Test run column in v2, do not extend the run-history columns by default
acceptance_rules: reuse the closest existing spec as template
categorize cases as P0, P1, or P2
do not unnecessarily retest lower-layer flows like login
create or update companion markdown with each spec
run the relevant priority set after generation
capture failures and update memory files
future coverage should be tagged and remain visible instead of being permanently skipped
default suites should exclude future-tagged tests rather than hiding them with long-term fixme/skip
feature-readiness coverage must not use permanent skip/fixme; keep it executable under @future
runtime/data blockers must be documented separately from future-feature coverage
default-behavior tests such as auto-select, prefill, and carry-forward must fail strictly when the default behavior is missing
independent downstream tests should establish their own valid prerequisites instead of cascading from an earlier default-selection failure
do not mark a generated spec as complete unless it has been run or an execution blocker has been documented
if a suite was generated earlier without a trustworthy execution attempt, move it back to revalidation status until rerun
environment_rules: environment aliases: local = localhost:4200, test = https://test.whizard.club, dev = https://dev.whizard.club
do not assume localhost by default when the bug source, manual testing source, or user wording points to test or dev
if manual testing was performed in a named environment, validate those findings in the same environment unless the user explicitly asks for a different one
if the target environment is unclear, ask for confirmation before running validation or reruns
for authenticated WRCF runs, verify frontend 4200 and backend ports 3000 and 3001 are reachable before debugging selectors
prefer explicit service-readiness checks in authenticated workbook-driven specs so backend outages fail fast
if service preflight passes but login still hangs, document the auth/runtime blocker separately instead of attributing it to selector issues
for local validation, treat localhost:4200 as the current behavior truth because it may include fixes newer than the local workspace code
if the confirmed environment behavior differs from checked-out source code, prefer the live UI behavior for validation notes and confirm before assuming the workspace code is current
large_run_rules: do not size rerun batches by sheet count alone
prefer rerun batches of about 100-120 Playwright tests per run
if the requested scope is much larger, split it into multiple runs of roughly 100-120 tests and tell the user that this is the recommended execution strategy
execution_phase_rules: during patching/debugging, fail-fast runs are allowed
when preparing the final issue for a sheet, rerun the full sheet without fail-fast
final issue content must be based on the full-sheet run, not on a partial debug run
if the user asks to create an issue and does not explicitly narrow scope, assume they want the full-sheet run result for that sheet
token_rules: do not restate full project background
work on the next pending entity or small batch
use PDFs as the primary product source
use the workbook as a derived coverage map
use tags/grouping to separate stable and future coverage without duplicating files
playwright_tag_rules: use module tags like @dashboard, @task, or @skills when they improve targeted runs
default Playwright execution should include @future unless the user explicitly asks to exclude it
explicit future-only runs can still use --grep @future when targeted coverage is needed
during normal reruns, run specs in the default way and include @future until the user says otherwise
github_issue_rules: before drafting or creating any GitHub issue, confirm the scope, environment, and whether the user wants one combined issue or separate issues
start the issue body with a heading: ## Run Summary
format the run summary fields with bold labels: **Environment**, **Suite** or **Suites**, **Result**
for each failed test add a heading line using the test id, for example: ## DASH-E2E-007
under each failed-test heading use this exact bold field order: **Test ID**, **Scenario**, **Expected Result**, **Likely Code Area**, **Probable Root Cause**, **Suggested Fix / Investigation Hint**
do not add extra introductory text unless the user explicitly asks for it
